name: into-thin-air
description: Jon Krakauer's Into Thin Air — mountaineering narrative exploring the 1996 Everest disaster, the commercialization of high-altitude climbing, human error under extreme pressure, the ethics of mountain guiding, and the psychology of survival
useCase: Understand the 1996 Everest disaster, learn from decision-making failures at extreme altitude, explore the ethics of commercial expeditions, and apply lessons about risk and survival
knownRisks: Krakauer's account has been criticized by some survivors (particularly Anatoli Boukreev) as one-sided
The book is memoir, not journalism — Krakauer was a participant, not an objective observer
Mountaineering lessons may not translate directly to other domains of risk
